title: moraea pavonia var. lutea
intro: Moraea pavonia var. lutea, also known as the Cape tulip or yellow-flowered Moraea, is a perennial plant species in the Iridaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid and glycoside content. The plant is believed to possess health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate conditions like arthritis and reduce oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, Moraea pavonia var. lutea is often used as a dried root or in infusions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
